preview_text:
The model favors Czech Republic at 51%, with South Africa given a 19% chance of victory. The 32-percentage-point gap reflects a clear home-field advantage in the model's assessment, though South Africa remains plausible as an underdog.

The 30% draw probability signals a moderately competitive match. Czech Republic is the statistical favorite, but the spread across outcomes—no single result dominating—suggests the contest carries genuine uncertainty. A stalemate ranks as the second-likeliest outcome, indicating the teams are not far apart in attacking or defensive capacity by the model's measure.

The model and sportsbook consensus diverge noticeably. Books assign Czech Republic 6 percentage points less winning probability than the model, while crediting South Africa 5.6 points more. The draw estimate is nearly identical across both sources. No Polymarket contract exists yet for this fixture.
